title: Rugged Canadian Beef Burger
description: A briny, chewy Canadian beef burger that marries garlic powder and a dash of Worcestershire sauce for a bold, dash-friendly flavor.
instructions: Preheat a cast‑iron skillet over medium‑high heat.
In a large bowl combine ground beef, garlic powder, salt, pepper, and Worcestershire sauce; mix until just combined.
Divide the mixture into 4 equal portions and shape each into a patty ¾ inch thick; let the patties rest at room temperature for 10 minutes.
Place the skillet on the stove and add a splash of oil; when it shimmers, add the patties.
Cook the patties 4–5 minutes on the first side until a crust forms; flip and cook an additional 3–4 minutes for medium doneness.
While the patties finish cooking, toast the buns cut‑side down in the skillet for 1 minute.
Remove the patties and buns; assemble by placing a lettuce leaf on the bottom bun, followed by the cooked patty, a tomato slice, and the top bun.
Serve immediately with a side of ketchup or mustard if desired.
Let the burgers rest for 2 minutes before cutting to retain juices.
Enjoy a rugged, briny Canadian beef burger that delivers a chewy bite and dash‑friendly flavor.
